title: Modern Angular (v17–v21)
description: The new-generation Angular: standalone components, built-in control flow, @defer, zoneless change detection, SSR & hydration, signal forms.

questions.question: What are standalone components, and why did Angular move away from NgModules as the default? What used to live in an NgModule, and where does that responsibility go in a standalone app?
Describe the built-in control flow syntax: `@if`, `@for` and `@switch`. What advantages does it have over `*ngIf`/`*ngFor`, and why does `@for` require a `track` expression?

What does a `@defer` block do? Explain the triggers (`on viewport`, `on interaction`, `when ...`), the `@placeholder`/`@loading`/`@error` sub-blocks, and what actually happens to the deferred code at build time.
How does classic zone.js-based change detection work — what does zone.js patch, and when does Angular decide to re-check components? What are the downsides that motivated moving away from it?

What is zoneless change detection (`provideZonelessChangeDetection`)? How does Angular know when to update the view without zone.js, and what does your code have to do differently for the UI to stay in sync?

Explain server-side rendering and hydration in Angular. What problem does hydration solve compared to destroy-and-re-render, and what do event replay and incremental hydration (`hydrate on ...` triggers on @defer blocks) add on top?

What are Signal Forms (the experimental signal-based forms API), and what pain points of Reactive Forms are they trying to fix — think typing, deriving validity as state, and synchronizing the model with the view?

How has Angular's toolchain modernized in recent versions? Cover the esbuild/Vite-based `application` builder replacing webpack, and the move of unit testing away from Karma (Vitest/Web Test Runner). What do these changes buy day-to-day?
